title: Every data-bearing survey row populates bind::oc:briefdescription
description: OC uses bind::oc:briefdescription in three places: annotated eCRF generation, Participant Matrix custom column headers, and item brief description in Insight. When unpopulated, OC falls back to the full label, which is often too long for table headers and produces poor annotated CRFs. DEFAULT: every survey row of type text, integer, decimal, date, select_one, select_multiple, or calculate populates bind::oc:briefdescription. EXCLUDED: note, begin group, end group, begin repeat, end repeat. Generation rule: for CDASH-mapped fields, use the standard CDASH label ('Subject ID', 'AE Start Date', 'AE Severity'); for custom fields, derive from question text — first 3-5 words, no trailing punctuation, sentence case. UNIVERSAL SCOPE per §0: applies regardless of definition_source. Both customer-sourced and CDASH-default rows get the auto-fill. Where the customer source already provides a briefdescription, that authored value wins; the auto-fill only populates rows where the field is empty.
rationale: Insight tables, the Participant Matrix, and annotated CRFs all use briefdescription as their column header. Without it, the full label is used — which for questions like 'Date the subject signed the Informed Consent Form' produces unreadable tables. The 3-5-word derivation from question text is the established pattern across forms; it preserves enough context to identify the field while fitting in column-header constraints. Universal scope (unlike §3/§4/§5) reflects the IE-6 walkthrough decision: briefdescription is purely additive metadata, not a structural override, so applying it to customer-sourced rows that lack it doesn't conflict with the customer authoring intent.

flag.message: Field ${field.name} on ${form.form_id} (type=${field.type}) requires bind__oc_briefdescription. For CDASH-mapped fields use the standard CDASH label; for custom fields derive 3-5 words from the question text in sentence case, no trailing punctuation.

history.note: Imported from conventions.md §13 during Phase B.1d. Structured: applies_when filters on field.type (only data-bearing types), effect uses require (raises flag if path is empty). Note the universal scope — applies regardless of form.definition_source, unlike §3/§4/§5 which gate on cdash_default. This reflects the IE-6 decision: briefdescription is additive metadata that doesn't conflict with customer authoring intent, so applying it universally is safe. The derivation rule (3-5 words from question text, sentence case) is delegated to Claude via the flag; a future DSL primitive could auto-derive from field.label.
